hero.headline: SSODL Online MBA in Business Analytics: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Symbiosis International University's fully-online MBA with a Business Analytics specialisation. NIRF #24 University, NAAC A++, UGC-DEB approved. Rs 55,000 work-experience scholarship for 2+ year professionals. HR Analytics, Spreadsheet Modelling, Visual Analytics, Social Media Analytics, Financial Analytics and Healthcare Analytics in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: SSODL Online MBA Business Analytics: Symbiosis brand (NIRF #24, NAAC A++), Rs 55K work-experience scholarship, broad analytics spec covering HR, Finance, Marketing, Healthcare and Social Media domains. Fees Rs 3.15L with scholarship, Rs 3.70L standard. Strongest for multi-domain analytics management roles.

about.body:
The SSODL Online MBA in Business Analytics is offered by Symbiosis School for Online and Digital Learning, the fully-online arm of Symbiosis International University (SIU), Pune. SSODL launched in 2020-21 as a UGC-DEB approved institution.

SIU carries NAAC A++ accreditation and NIRF #24 University ranking (2025). The Business Analytics specialisation is distinctively cross-domain: Sem 3 covers HR Analytics, Spreadsheet Modelling, Visual Analytics, and Social Media Analytics, while Sem 4 adds Marketing and Sales Analytics, Financial Analytics, Healthcare Analytics, and Business Forecasting. This multi-domain coverage makes SSODL Business Analytics graduates valuable in analytics consulting, business intelligence, and data management roles across sectors.

The Sem 1-2 core already includes AI and ML for Business Management, R Programming, and Data Driven Decision Making, giving the analytics specialisation graduates strong foundational data skills before they reach the domain-specific Sem 3-4 subjects.

sem1Subjects.name: Business Statistics
sem1Subjects.description: Apply descriptive statistics, hypothesis testing, and regression analysis as the quantitative foundation for analytics work.

sem1Subjects.name: Marketing Management
sem1Subjects.description: Understand marketing data structures, campaign performance metrics, and customer acquisition analytics frameworks.

sem1Subjects.name: Financial Accounting
sem1Subjects.description: Read financial statements and accounting data as inputs for financial analytics and business performance dashboards.

sem1Subjects.name: Operations Management
sem1Subjects.description: Apply process performance metrics, OEE, and supply chain data concepts to operations analytics.

sem1Subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
sem1Subjects.description: Apply data privacy law, PDPB compliance, and analytics ethics frameworks to business analytics practice.

sem1Subjects.name: Human Resource Management
sem1Subjects.description: Understand HR data structures and workforce metrics as inputs for people analytics assignments.

sem1Subjects.name: Research Methodology
sem1Subjects.description: Design research studies, select analytical methods, and structure data collection for business analytics projects.

sem1Subjects.name: Microeconomics
sem1Subjects.description: Apply demand modelling, price elasticity, and market structure data to economic analytics and pricing research.

sem1Subjects.name: Technology in Business
sem1Subjects.description: Evaluate analytics platforms: Tableau, Power BI, Google Analytics, and cloud data infrastructure for business intelligence.

sem1Subjects.name: Data Driven Decision Making
sem1Subjects.description: Build data-driven decision frameworks and analytics governance principles for organisational use.

sem2Subjects.name: Organizational Behaviour
sem2Subjects.description: Apply behavioural data insights and people analytics to team performance and organisational design.

sem2Subjects.name: Operations Research
sem2Subjects.description: Apply linear programming, simulation, and optimisation models to business analytics problem-solving.

sem2Subjects.name: Macroeconomics
sem2Subjects.description: Interpret macroeconomic data series for business forecasting and economic indicator analysis.

sem2Subjects.name: Financial Management
sem2Subjects.description: Apply financial ratio analysis and capital structure data to financial analytics dashboards.

sem2Subjects.name: R Programming
sem2Subjects.description: Build statistical models, data cleaning scripts, and visualisation outputs using R for analytics assignments.

sem2Subjects.name: Project Management
sem2Subjects.description: Manage analytics project timelines, stakeholder deliverables, and data pipeline implementation.

sem2Subjects.name: Management Information Systems
sem2Subjects.description: Design business intelligence system architecture and evaluate MIS for enterprise analytics.

sem2Subjects.name: Business Communication
sem2Subjects.description: Present analytics findings to non-technical stakeholders through data storytelling and executive summaries.

sem2Subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour and Insights
sem2Subjects.description: Apply consumer data analysis, segmentation models, and purchase pattern analytics to marketing decisions.

sem2Subjects.name: Digital Marketing
sem2Subjects.description: Use digital analytics platforms for campaign performance measurement, SEO data analysis, and attribution modelling.

sem2Subjects.name: Design Thinking
sem2Subjects.description: Apply human-centred design to analytics dashboard UX, data product development, and insight communication.

sem2Subjects.name: Innovation Management
sem2Subjects.description: Evaluate analytics innovation: AI-driven product development, data monetisation models, and analytics-led business transformation.

sem3Subjects.name: Project I (4 Credits)
sem3Subjects.description: Conduct an analytics research project applying one or more domain analytics tools to a real business dataset.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Governance and Ethics
sem3Subjects.description: Apply data governance standards, analytics ethics, and responsible AI frameworks to business analytics practice.

sem3Subjects.name: Crisis Management in Business
sem3Subjects.description: Use analytics to detect early warning signals, monitor KPIs during crises, and support data-driven response.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply competitive intelligence analytics, market sizing data, and strategic KPI frameworks to business strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: AI and ML for Business Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply supervised and unsupervised ML models, neural network concepts, and AI product evaluation to management decisions.

sem3Subjects.name: HR Analytics
sem3Subjects.description: Build attrition prediction models, workforce dashboards, and compensation band analysis using people data.

sem3Subjects.name: Spreadsheet Modelling
sem3Subjects.description: Build advanced Excel and Google Sheets models for financial, operational, and forecasting analytics.

sem3Subjects.name: Visual Analytics
sem3Subjects.description: Design Tableau and Power BI dashboards for business performance monitoring and executive reporting.

sem3Subjects.name: Social Media Analytics
sem3Subjects.description: Measure social media campaign reach, sentiment analysis, influencer ROI, and brand mention tracking using analytics tools.

sem4Subjects.name: Project II (10 Credits)
sem4Subjects.description: Complete a full analytics dissertation applying multi-domain analytics methodology to a real business problem.

sem4Subjects.name: Entrepreneurship
sem4Subjects.description: Build an analytics-driven startup pitch: data product design, analytics business model, and go-to-market strategy.

sem4Subjects.name: Global Business Environment
sem4Subjects.description: Apply international market analytics and cross-border data regulation to global business analytics strategy.

sem4Subjects.name: Conflict and Negotiation
sem4Subjects.description: Use data in negotiations: analytics-backed proposals, data-driven contract terms, and evidence-based stakeholder persuasion.

sem4Subjects.name: Doing Business in India
sem4Subjects.description: Apply India-specific data regulations, sector analytics benchmarks, and regional market data to Indian business analytics.

sem4Subjects.name: Business Transformation and Organizational Turnaround
sem4Subjects.description: Use analytics to support business transformation decisions, track transformation KPIs, and measure organisational change impact.

sem4Subjects.name: Marketing and Sales Analytics
sem4Subjects.description: Apply attribution modelling, sales funnel analytics, customer lifetime value models, and marketing mix modelling.

sem4Subjects.name: Financial Analytics
sem4Subjects.description: Build financial performance dashboards, fraud detection models, and investment analytics using financial data.

sem4Subjects.name: Healthcare Analytics
sem4Subjects.description: Apply patient outcome analytics, hospital performance benchmarking, and health data analysis to healthcare management.

sem4Subjects.name: Business Forecasting
sem4Subjects.description: Apply time-series models, demand forecasting techniques, and scenario planning analytics to business planning.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all SSODL MBA specialisations. The Business Analytics specialisation adds 9 subjects in Sem 3 and 10 subjects in Sem 4, covering HR, Social Media, Marketing, Finance, Healthcare, and Forecasting analytics domains.

sections.whoHires:
Business Analytics MBA graduates from SSODL Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** IT services and consulting, BFSI analytics, E-commerce, Healthcare technology, FMCG analytics, Management consulting

**Entry-level roles:** Business Analyst, Data Analyst, Analytics Consultant, BI Analyst, Marketing Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Analytics Manager, Head of Business Intelligence, Senior Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Analytics, Head of Data and Analytics, Chief Data Officer, Analytics Practice Head

**Top hiring organisations:** Infosys, TCS, Accenture, Deloitte Analytics, KPMG Data and Analytics, IBM Analytics, Amazon India, Flipkart, HDFC Bank Analytics, Kotak Bank Analytics, Swiggy, Zomato
sections.skills:
The SSODL Business Analytics specialisation builds multi-domain analytics skills that management consulting and business intelligence employers specifically seek:

**Technical skills:**
- Visual Analytics: Tableau and Power BI dashboard design, executive reporting, and data visualisation for non-technical audiences
- Spreadsheet Modelling: advanced Excel and Google Sheets financial, operational, and forecasting model construction
- Social Media Analytics: campaign reach measurement, sentiment analysis, influencer ROI tracking, and brand mention monitoring
- Business Forecasting: time-series modelling, demand forecasting, and scenario planning for strategic planning teams

**Applied skills:**
- Multi-domain Analytics: HR Analytics, Marketing and Sales Analytics, Financial Analytics, and Healthcare Analytics covering four distinct business domains in a single programme
- AI and ML for Business Management: ML model evaluation, AI product assessment, and analytics platform decision-making for management roles

**Soft skills:** Presenting analytics findings to non-technical boards, translating data insights into business recommendations, managing analytics project stakeholders, and building business cases for analytics investment.
sections.comparisons:
Comparing SSODL Online MBA Business Analytics against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Business Analytics:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Business Analytics focuses on Power BI, supervised ML, and domain analytics across business functions. SSODL Business Analytics covers a broader domain set: HR Analytics, Social Media Analytics, Healthcare Analytics, and Financial Analytics as dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects. Amity is more affordable. SSODL carries stronger Pune-region brand recognition. For pure cost efficiency, Amity is the stronger value.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences Management:** NMIMS Ops and Data Sciences combines supply chain management depth with analytics. SSODL Business Analytics is purer in analytics focus: no supply chain, but deeper in social media, HR, and healthcare analytics domains. Choose SSODL for cross-domain analytics management; NMIMS for supply chain analytics specifically.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Analytics and Data Science:** MUJ is NAAC A+ and NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000-1,80,000. MUJ Analytics covers Python, R, and advanced ML with a more technically deep data science credential. SSODL Business Analytics is broader in domain coverage and more management-oriented. For technical data scientist roles, MUJ Analytics is more targeted. For analytics management across multiple business domains, SSODL is more applicable.

fees.body:
The SSODL Online MBA in Business Analytics offers 2 fee tracks based on applicant work experience:

**Track 1 — Standard (under 2 years work experience)**:
- Application fee: Rs 1,000
- Registration fee: Rs 10,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 3,70,000 across 4 semesters
- Semester breakdown: Rs 92,500 each for Sem 1-2 and Rs 87,500 each for Sem 3-4
- Year 1 total: Rs 1,95,000 (including Rs 10,000 registration)
- Year 2 total: Rs 1,75,000

**Track 2 — With Work Experience Scholarship (2+ years experience)**:
- Application fee: Rs 1,000
- Registration fee: Rs 10,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 3,15,000 after Rs 55,000 experience-based scholarship
- Semester breakdown: Rs 80,000 each for Sem 1-2 and Rs 72,500 each for Sem 3-4
- Year 1 total: Rs 1,70,000
- Year 2 total: Rs 1,45,000

The Rs 55,000 experience scholarship applies whether you pay semester-wise or opt for one-time upfront payment. Submit 2+ years work experience proof during admission to claim.

EMI is available through the university's marketing partner. This is interest-bearing EMI, not zero-cost. Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options and approval terms.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with SSODL each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and scholarship eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Symbiosis International University (SIU) and carries the SIU name with the MBA designation. The Business Analytics specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India. SSODL is the UGC-DEB approved online MBA arm of SIU, distinct from SCDL.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from SSODL Online MBA Business Analytics alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Vikram Nair
items.liked: The multi-domain analytics coverage was genuinely useful. Visual Analytics with Tableau and Spreadsheet Modelling subjects were directly applicable to my consulting role. The Symbiosis brand got me shortlisted for analytics positions at Deloitte.
items.disliked: Healthcare Analytics felt lightweight compared to the rest. A deeper dataset-based project would have helped.
items.body: I had 3 years in IT analytics support and wanted to move into analytics management. SSODL Business Analytics gave me both the credential and domain breadth. The Rs 55K scholarship brought fees within range. Landed a BI Analyst role at a Pune consulting firm within 5 months.

items.name: Shreya Bhatt
items.liked: R Programming in Sem 2 and Business Forecasting in Sem 4 were the most valuable for my financial analytics career. Social Media Analytics was a useful surprise.
items.disliked: Fees are the highest among online MBA analytics options. Interest-bearing EMI adds significant cost over 2 years.
items.body: Good programme for analytics generalists. If you are already technical (Python, SQL), the Spreadsheet Modelling subject may feel basic. But for management-track analytics roles, the domain breadth and Symbiosis brand combination is strong.

items.name: Nikhil Patel
items.liked: Visual Analytics with Power BI was excellent. Multi-domain coverage stands out compared to single-domain analytics MBAs.
items.disliked: Limited Python coverage. For Bengaluru data analytics hiring, Python is expected. Had to self-learn alongside the programme.
items.body: Solid programme but the tech depth is lower than MUJ Analytics or dedicated data science programmes. If your target role is analytics manager rather than data scientist, SSODL is suitable. For pure data science, look elsewhere.

faqs.question: How does SSODL's work-experience scholarship work?
faqs.answer: Applicants with 2 or more years of documented work experience receive a Rs 55,000 scholarship off the standard Rs 3,70,000 program fee. The scholarship applies whether you pay semester-wise or in a single upfront payment. Submit proof of work experience during admission to have it applied automatically.

faqs.question: Is SSODL Online MBA the same as Symbiosis on-campus MBA?
faqs.answer: Both are awarded by Symbiosis International University. SSODL Online MBA is UGC-DEB approved and fully online. The SIU name appears on both certificates. Employers treat UGC-DEB approved online MBAs as equivalent for corporate hiring eligibility.

faqs.question: Does SSODL offer no-cost EMI?
faqs.answer: No. SSODL EMI is interest-bearing and routed through the university's marketing partner. MUJ and NMIMS offer zero-cost EMI. Factor in EMI interest cost when comparing total programme cost.

faqs.question: Does SSODL Business Analytics include Python or only R?
faqs.answer: The core curriculum includes R Programming in Sem 2. Python is not a dedicated subject. Sem 3-4 analytics subjects focus on domain analytics: HR, Social Media, Marketing, Finance, and Healthcare analytics using management-oriented tools. For Python-heavy data science roles, a dedicated data science programme may be more appropriate.

faqs.question: Which roles is SSODL Business Analytics most competitive for?
faqs.answer: The multi-domain coverage makes SSODL Business Analytics strongest for analytics manager, business intelligence head, and analytics consulting roles where domain breadth matters more than technical depth. Sectors like management consulting, BFSI analytics, and healthcare BI value the cross-domain profile.

chooseIf: You want multi-domain analytics coverage: HR, Social Media, Marketing, Finance, and Healthcare Analytics in a single programme.
You have 2+ years work experience and the Rs 55,000 scholarship reduces effective fees to Rs 3.15L.
You want analytics management roles (BI head, analytics manager) rather than pure data scientist technical roles.
consider: If Python and advanced ML are required for your target role, MUJ Online MBA Analytics and Data Science covers Python and advanced ML at a lower fee.
If fee is the primary concern, Amity Online MBA Business Analytics (Rs 2.25L) covers core analytics at a significantly lower cost.
If zero-cost EMI matters, MUJ and NMIMS offer zero-cost EMI; SSODL does not.
